steps.title: Factor the second denominator
steps.content: Start with $$\frac{y+12}{x-8}+\frac{y(x-8)}{x^2y-8xy}$$ The second denominator factors as $$x^2y-8xy=xy(x-8)$$ so the first fraction needs to be multiplied by $\frac{xy}{xy}$ to get the same denominator.

steps.title: Combine the numerators
steps.content: With the common denominator, the expression becomes $$\frac{xy(y+12)}{xy(x-8)}+\frac{y(x-8)}{xy(x-8)}$$ Combine the numerators: $$\frac{xy(y+12)+y(x-8)}{xy(x-8)}$$ Distribute in the numerator: $$xy^2+12xy+xy-8y=xy^2+13xy-8y$$

steps.title: Match the final form
steps.content: The denominator $xy(x-8)$ is the same as $x^2y-8xy$, so the simplified expression is $$\frac{xy^2+13xy-8y}{x^2y-8xy}$$ That matches Choice C — $\frac{x{y}^{2}+13xy-8y}{{x}^{2}y-8xy}$.
